description:
Target repo: beadwork CLI (~/src/beadwork; remotes iautom8things/beadwork upstream, jallum/beadwork origin).

Problem
`bw comment <id> <text>` takes the body only as a positional arg, with no flag to read it from a file or stdin. `bw comment --help` lists only `-a/--author` and `--json` — there is NO `-F/--file`. When a scripted/automated caller builds a command that lands a flag-looking token (e.g. `-F`) in the <text> position, bw SILENTLY accepts the bare `-F` as the comment text and drops the intended (often multi-line) body. No error is raised.

Observed impact
During an /orchestrate-epic run (mutagen-835, ~13 subagent verify/audit cycles), multiple worker subagents lost their entire VERIFY:/AUDIT: comment bodies — only a literal "-F" landed on the ticket (see e.g. several stray "-F" comments). Each lost verdict had to be reconstructed (re-verify / re-audit / inline confirmation). Multi-line comment bodies are exactly what graders, CI bots, and LLM agents post, so this is a sharp edge for any automated bw user.

Requested fixes (either/both)
1. Add `-F, --file <path>` to `bw comment`, and support `-F -` to read the body from stdin — mirroring `gh issue comment -F/--body-file` and `git commit -F`. This gives scripted callers a robust way to pass multi-line bodies without shell-quoting/heredoc fragility.
2. Make arg parsing reject (error on) an unknown `-`-prefixed token in the <text> position instead of silently treating it as comment text. A lone `-F` should fail loudly ("unknown flag -F; did you mean --file?"), not post "-F".

Repro
  bw comment <id> -F            # posts a comment whose text is literally "-F" (expected: error)
  bw comment <id> -F body.txt   # no --file support; "-F" becomes the text, "body.txt" is an extra arg

Related: existing plan-board ticket to upgrade bw to 0.13.2.

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED

Session: f5541799-15fe-434b-b461-54848a9f6981
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/f5541799-15fe-434b-b461-54848a9f6981.jsonl

Target repo: ~/src/beadwork (Go CLI). Branch: bw-10h/comment-file-flag (off main). Commit: 0361ad3

## Summary
Both requested fixes landed.

1. Added `-F, --file <path>` to `bw comment`. `--file -` reads the body from
   stdin (mirrors `gh issue comment -F` / `git commit -F`). Trailing newlines
   are trimmed; internal blank lines and leading whitespace preserved.
2. `ParseArgs` now rejects any unrecognized `-`-prefixed token (single- OR
   double-dash) instead of silently swallowing it as a positional. So a stray
   `-F` (or `-x`) in the `<text>` slot fails loudly ("unknown flag: -x") rather
   than being posted as the comment body.

Escape hatches preserved so the reject doesn't over-fire:
- bare `-` stays a positional (the stdin sentinel — `bw import -` still works).
- `--` terminates flag parsing, so a body that legitimately starts with a dash
  is postable: `bw comment <id> -- "-1 looks like a flag"`.

`bw comment` also now rejects: body given twice (`<text>` AND `--file`), an
empty body, and surplus positionals (unquoted multi-word text) — all of which
were prior silent-drop sharp edges of the same class.

## Files touched
- cmd/bw/comments.go    — --file/-F + stdin, body resolution, validation
- cmd/bw/helpers.go     — ParseArgs: reject stray flags; bare - / -- handling
- cmd/bw/command.go     — comment Command: --file flag, examples, help, optional <text>
- cmd/bw/comments_test.go — 8 new command-level tests
- cmd/bw/helpers_test.go  — 5 new ParseArgs tests

## Tests added (13)
comments_test.go: FromFile, FromFileShortFlag, FromStdin,
  BareFlagInTextPositionRejected, UnknownFlagInTextPositionRejected,
  TextAndFileConflict, LeadingDashBodyViaDoubleDash, EmptyFileRejected
helpers_test.go: RejectsUnknownShortFlag, RejectsUnknownLongFlag,
  BareDashIsPositional, DoubleDashEndsFlagParsing, DashValueConsumedNotRejected

## Verification (Phase 1)
- go build ./... — clean
- go vet ./cmd/bw — clean
- make staticcheck — clean
- go test ./cmd/bw -run 'Comment|ParseArgs' -count=1 — PASS (incl. preserved
  TestParseArgsValueFlagAtEnd; old silent-trailing-value-flag behavior kept,
  not changed, to stay in scope)
- Real-binary repro in an isolated temp repo:
    bw comment <id> -F            -> error, exit 1, nothing posted
    bw comment <id> -F body.txt   -> reads file, posts multi-line body
    printf ... | bw comment <id> --file -  -> reads stdin
    bw comment <id> -x            -> "unknown flag: -x"
    echo {...} | bw import -       -> still imports (bare-dash regression OK)
    bw label <id> -wontfix         -> still removes label (own parser)

## Pre-existing failures (NOT introduced here)
Full `go test ./cmd/bw` shows 3 FAILs — TestListEmptyOutput, TestUpgradeRepoCLI,
TestImportRoundtrip — all in cli_test.go. Verified they fail identically on
pristine `main` (stash applied): they pick up a real "Upgrade bw to 0.13.2"
issue leaking from the host registry. This is the test-isolation gap the
in-flight BW_REGISTRY_AUTO=0 WIP addresses; orthogonal to this ticket.

## Deferred must requirements: none

Elixir test-quality gate: N/A (Go repo).

VERIFY: PASS
Session: f5541799-15fe-434b-b461-54848a9f6981
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/f5541799-15fe-434b-b461-54848a9f6981.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 5 (command.go, comments.go, comments_test.go, helpers.go, helpers_test.go)
- Files outside intended scope: 0. All five are the comment command def, the comment handler, the shared arg parser, and their two test files — squarely on the ticket's intent (fix bw comment arg handling). Note: NOT a spec-led ticket (no Advances:/.spec/Allowed-touches), so spec-stub steps are N/A and were skipped.

Semantic checks:
- Project verification: go build ./... PASS; go vet ./cmd/bw PASS; make staticcheck PASS; go test ./cmd/bw -run 'Comment|ParseArgs' -count=1 PASS.
- Pre-existing cli_test failures assessment: TestListEmptyOutput, TestUpgradeRepoCLI, TestImportRoundtrip FAIL on this branch — and FAIL IDENTICALLY on a pristine 'main' worktree (same 'Upgrade bw to 0.13.2' issue leaking from the host registry into test repos). Confirmed pre-existing host-registry pollution, NOT a regression from this branch. This branch does not touch cli_test.go.
- Requirements coverage:
  - Fix 1 (--file/-F/stdin): covered by comments_test.go:96 (FromFile), :120 (-F short flag), :140 (--file - stdin), + helpers_test.go:163 (dash value not rejected). Would fail if readCommentBody stopped reading commentStdin for path '-', if expandAliases dropped -F->--file, or if --file were not registered as a value flag. Traced: commentStdin (comments.go:13) is genuinely read in readCommentBody and swapped by the stdin test; -F->--file mapping verified via expandAliases value-flag entry.
  - Fix 2 (reject stray flag tokens): covered by comments_test.go:159 (bare -F errors AND stores nothing), :175 (-x), + helpers_test.go:118/:128 (short/long unknown rejected; :118 asserts the error names -F). Would fail if ParseArgs reverted to swallowing single-dash tokens as positionals (helpers.go:172-173 now returns 'unknown flag: %s').
  - Regression guard (bare -, --, label -wontfix, import -): bare - covered by helpers_test.go:138 + exempted at helpers.go:159; -- end-of-flags by helpers_test.go:149 + comments_test.go LeadingDashBodyViaDoubleDash; import - by import_test.go:112 (cmdImport routes bare - to stdin, unaffected); label -wontfix uses its OWN parser (label.go:24-34, TrimPrefix), NOT ParseArgs, so it is structurally immune.
- Intent-level concerns: none. Audited all 22 non-test ParseArgs callers — every one consumes only --double-dash flags and non-dash positionals; the sole single-dash positional in the codebase is the 'import -' stdin sentinel, which is explicitly exempted. No command passes negative-number bare positionals. recap pre-expands -v->--verbose before ParseArgs. The new single-dash reject is well contained.

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only): The implementer also added in-scope guards (body-given-twice, empty body, surplus positionals) with tests; these are same-class silent-drop fixes and are fine. The 3 cli_test.go failures are a real but orthogonal test-isolation gap (the in-flight BW_REGISTRY_AUTO=0 WIP addresses it) — worth landing separately so the full suite goes green, but it does not gate this ticket.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: f5541799-15fe-434b-b461-54848a9f6981
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/f5541799-15fe-434b-b461-54848a9f6981.jsonl

Reasoning: Re-derived independently, not on faith.

Fix 1 (-F/--file + stdin): comments_test.go TestCmdCommentFromFile/FromFileShortFlag/FromStdin assert the stored body; FromStdin swaps the commentStdin package var (comments.go:14) proving readCommentBody (comments.go:62) is wired into the --file - path. Would fail if readCommentBody stopped reading commentStdin for '-' or expandAliases dropped -F->--file. Confirmed live against the built binary: -F body.txt and --file - both posted correct multi-line bodies (trailing \n trimmed, internal blanks preserved).

Fix 2 (reject stray flag in <text>): the change is in shared ParseArgs (helpers.go:159-173) — bare '-' and non-dash stay positional, '--' ends flag parsing, every other -prefixed token errors 'unknown flag'. helpers_test.go:118 asserts the error names -F (genuine binding: -F is NOT in the global aliases map, so it exercises the new single-dash path; reverting makes -F a positional and the test fails). Live binary: bare -F and -x both error exit 1, nothing posted; -- escape posts a leading-dash body verbatim; both-text-and-file and empty-file both error.

Blast radius (re-derived, all 22 ParseArgs callers): pre-change, single-dash non-aliased tokens already became positionals; the aliased shorts (-p/-t/-a/-d/-s/-l/-g/-y) already errored for non-registering commands, so only single-dash non-aliased positionals change behavior. The sole such usage is import's stdin sentinel '-' (exempted at helpers.go:159; verified live: echo {json} | bw import - imports OK) and label -wontfix, which uses its OWN parser parseLabelArgs (label.go:19, never ParseArgs; verified live: bw label <id> -wontfix exit 0). The only single-dash-positional example in the entire command catalog is 'bw label ... -wontfix' (command.go:292). No command passes negative-number bare positionals. recap pre-expands -v before ParseArgs.

Mechanical: git diff main..HEAD --stat shows exactly 5 files (command.go, comments.go, helpers.go + 2 test files), matching VERIFY. The 3 cli_test.go failures (TestListEmptyOutput, TestUpgradeRepoCLI, TestImportRoundtrip) fail IDENTICALLY on a pristine main worktree (same 'Upgrade bw to 0.13.2' host-registry leak) — confirmed not a regression. go build/vet/staticcheck clean; go test -run 'Comment|ParseArgs' all PASS.

title: bw comment: add -F/--file (or stdin) for body; reject bare -F silently posted as comment text
